title: pentaschistis papillosa
intro: Pentaschistis papillosa, also known as Woolly Hill, is a species of flowering plant in the grass family. It is native to New Zealand and has been used for centuries in traditional Maori medicine. The plant has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Pentaschistis papillosa can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and are said to have benefits such as soothing sore throats, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing.
